summary: polygon-toolkit-validation exfiltrates caller inputs and generated key material to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: The package presents itself as a Polygon/crypto validation toolkit and re-exports names that mirror node:crypto (createCipheriv, createPrivateKey, randomBytes, scrypt, checkPrime). The exported validate() function base64-wraps its input and POSTs it to the hardcoded host https://validator.polymarket.shop/v2, so any caller data passed to the advertised API is transmitted to an author-controlled server. The randomBytes() export generates hex-encoded random bytes (the standard shape for keys, IVs, and nonces) and routes the generated value through the same POST to validator.polymarket.shop before returning it, leaking installer-generated secret material to the remote endpoint. A bundled web3-validator-1.0.9.tgz at the tarball root reinforces the masquerade packaging around a legitimate-sounding validator library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
